calendar.matters:
Petition To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow.
Off Calendar Apr-24-2015 Text Ruling

Petition To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow.
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Continued To Apr-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Gilg, for and with Sarah and Melvin Booker; Gouri Nair for Sarah Hill (by CourtCall)

Prior to the commencement of the hearing, Ms. Gouri submitted to the Court a doctor's note restricting her travel due to infection, a copy of which was provided to Mr. Gilg.
Ms. Gouri requested a two week continuance of the hearing, to permit recovery from her infection and to allow her to be present in court for the evidentiary hearing. The Court agreed to hear argument on the issue of the need for filing of a creditor's claim, which issue was fully briefed, with the taking of evidence on the amount of the claim to occur at a later date.
The Court announced its tentative ruling thata creditor's claim is not required under sections 9391 and 1061, and therefore the statute of limitations defense to the claim of the Bookers fails.
Ms. Nair argued that timely filing of a creditor's claim was a prerequisite to recovery on the Booker claim, and that since she demanded the filing of a creditor's claim and the Bookers had failed to file one, the statute of limitations applied to bar the claim.
Mr. Gilg argued that no creditor's claim was required under the undisputed circumstances before the Court, and that waiver and estoppel additionally applied to bar the statute of limitations defense.
The Court ruled that the Bookers are entitled to recovery under the escrow, are not barred by the statute of limitations as the filing of a creditor's claim was not required under the statute, and that equitable estoppel would bar denial of the payment obligation. The only remaining issue is the amount due them.
Ms. Nair stated that there is a fact issue regarding the $30,000.00 deed of trust and its reconveyance for which an evidentiary hearing is necessary. She advised the Court that she would not be comfortable proceeding telephonically with an evidentiary hearing on that issue, as she would not be able to see the witnesses during their testimony.
The Court continued the balance of the hearing to April 25, 2015 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.
The Court stated that the hearing will be limited to determining the balance payable to the Bookers. Mr. Gilg requested leave to add one or more additional witnesses to his witness list for that hearing. The Court granted the request, setting a deadline of April 10, 2015 for identification of the witnesses in writing to Ms. Nair and the Court. The Court also gave Ms. Nair the deadline of April 17, 2015, to identify in writing to Mr. Gilg and the Court any witnesses she will require to respond to the additional witness(es) identified by Mr. Gilg.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy E. Lopes. (Not Reported)

Petition To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow.
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Continued To Feb-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Gilg for Melvin Booker and Sara Booker. Gouri Nair appeared by telephone for Jeri Hill. Eileen Hochstein for Shaomie Chen Kuang and Jian Zhen Kuang (Creditors/Purchasers). Michan Evonc (holds power of attorney for the Bookers) also present.

The Court stated that the petition seems to be based on Probate Code 9830 and that it is not at all clear a creditor's claim is necessary when there is a security interest. The Court also informed Mr. Evonc that because he is not an attorney he is not allowed to speak on behalf of the Bookers and that it Mr. Gilg's role to represent their interest. Furthermore, the Court informed Ms. Hochstein that there is nothing before the Court to be addressed with respect to her Clients' creditor's claim. Ms. Nouri stated that it is their position that the administrator does not need to pay off the secondary mortgage, that the funds are currently still being held in escrow and that the parties were advised to file a creditors claim timely. Mr. Gilg stated that the Bookers are not in good health, that they need the money and that they would like to get the matter resolved. Ms. Nouri stated that her Client is amenable to mediation and suggested setting a trial. The Petition to Distribute Funds Held in Escrow was set for trial on February 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties need to meet and confer to see if they can stipulate as to facts (amounts due/factual issues). If petitioners have anything to say about the creditor's claim issue, oppositions are to be filed by January 30, 2015 and replies need to be filed by February 13, 2015. Witness lists with expected time for testimony, exhibit lists and trial briefs are to be filed by February 20, 2015.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lelu. (Not Reported)

Petition To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow.
Off Calendar Dec-23-2014 Continued To Jan-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow.
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Continued To Dec-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Distribute Funds Held In Escrow
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Denied

Motion Regarding Disputed Fund Held In Escrow
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gouri Nair appeared by Court Call for Jeri Hill (Administrator). William Gilg for and with the Bookers. Michan Evonc (Person assisting the Bookers).

The parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to filing/not filing of a creditor's claim. Upon conclusion of argument, The Court denied the Motion Regarding Disputed Funds in Escrow without prejudice (The Court will issue an order). The Court further indicated that what the Administrator is asking for is a Petition for Distribution. Discussions were held with regard to what is the next step. Mr. Gilg stated that he will file a Creditor's Claim.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Not Reported.
